title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Design Defense
summary: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an MCP security test suite and operating policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Treat external resource and tool content as untrusted data that cannot redefine authority.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

# Add typed, bounded protocol increments below.

steps.title: Server Trust · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Server Trust without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.

content.text: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Server Trust, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an MCP security test suite and operating policy.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A listed community MCP server is officially verified. This fails the Server Trust contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Security Operations applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Patch, rotate, revoke, audit, rate-limit, and incident-test MCP deployments.
